description_long: This bungalow is a single-story residential building with a sprawling, asymmetrical footprint and multiple roof sections. The roof is predominantly hip-style with intersecting ridges, covered in brownish shingles, and features a central white section that may indicate a skylight or clerestory. The exterior walls are dark, likely wood or painted siding, punctuated by several rectangular windows and a few prominent chimneys. A small, covered porch with a gabled roof is visible at the front, providing a sheltered entryway. The windows are arranged irregularly, with some grouped and others spaced apart, contributing to the informal, homey character. The structure sits on a flat, patterned base, suggesting a suburban or neighborhood context.
description: A single-story, hip-roofed bungalow with dark siding, brown shingle roofs, and an asymmetrical layout featuring a small front porch and irregularly spaced windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a small gabled porch and several windows on the facade.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, sprawling layout, multiple roof sections, and a prominent central chimney.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back section of the bungalow, with more windows and the continuation of the hip roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the extended wings of the bungalow and additional windows, as well as the porch from a different angle.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped and spaced windows
